R1.question: In what country is Normandy located?
When were the Normans in Normandy?
R1.answer:
The Normans were in Normandy primarily during the 10th and 11th centuries. Their distinct cultural and ethnic identity emerged initially in the first half of the 10th century and continued to evolve over the succeeding centuries. They descended from Norse raiders who, under Rollo, swore fealty to King Charles III of West Francia and assimilated with the native Frankish and Roman-Gaulish populations.

(Note: The context also mentions their activities beyond Normandy in later periods, but their presence and identity formation in Normandy are centered on the 10th and 11th centuries.)

R1.question: From which countries did the Norse originate?
Who was the Norse leader?
What century did the Normans first gain their separate identity?
Who was the duke in the battle of Hastings?
R1.answer: William II of Normandy (also known as William the Conqueror)

R1.question: Who ruled the duchy of Normandy
R1.answer:
Rollo, Richard I of Normandy, and William II of Normandy (William the Conqueror) ruled the Duchy of Normandy. Rollo established it via the Treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte in 911; Richard I forged it into a cohesive principality; William II conquered England in 1066 as Duke of Normandy.
